round_trip_ping.note: 41.47µs — did not meet <30µs target. The 12µs gap is dominated by QUIC protocol processing and kqueue I/O overhead, not crypto or scheduling.

aead_encrypt_1kb_aes256gcm.note: AES-256-GCM with hardware ARMv8 AES — 1.5x faster than ChaCha20

runtime_comparison.reason: Quinn uses background I/O threads; multi_thread lets I/O thread handle kqueue while worker runs app logic

syscall_profile.actual_io_fast: QUIC protocol processing too fast to appear in sample

target_assessment.aead_1kb_target: <0.3µs — NOT MET for 1KB (1.10µs with hardware AES), MET for 64B (228ns)
target_assessment.syscall_target: <2 per message — NOT MEASURABLE precisely (dtrace requires sudo)

remaining_bottleneck_analysis.primary: QUIC protocol processing (quinn_proto) — frame encoding, ACK processing, flow control
remaining_bottleneck_analysis.secondary: kqueue I/O overhead — kevent registration and polling
remaining_bottleneck_analysis.tertiary: TLS record layer — even with hardware AES, TLS framing adds overhead
remaining_bottleneck_analysis.not_bottleneck: Crypto (1.1µs AEAD), serialization (138ns CBOR), scheduling (24ns yield), allocations (0/msg send)

cumulative_improvement.tracks_contributing: G (zero-copy), H (lock-free), I (connection pool), J (QUIC tuning), K (simd-json), L (hardware AES)
